data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this keystone child; no per-criterion coverage gap attributable to 8937 itself:
- Parent AC1 ("A shared, single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema exists; both producer output and consumer reads validate against it") → 8937 AC1 (_snapshot_schema.py defines the snapshot-entry shape as TypedDict + JSON Schema, single source of truth) + AC2 (test validates fetcher.fetch_snapshot output against the schema; would FAIL pre-fix — issuelinks absent / comment under wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC2 ("One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") → 8937 AC3 (round-trip test fetcher → differs → applier through the production path, asserts comment/issuelinks/parent + scalar fields read correctly; fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read), consuming fe57's real-payload fixtures (8937 depends on fe57). FULLY.
- Parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → 8937 AC1+AC2+AC3 pin and jointly exercise the shape; reinforced by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). FULLY.
- Contributes to SC5/AC6 posture via 8937 AC4 (all assertions semantic — keys/types/round-trip — not whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count).

DELEGATION — criteria 8937 does not own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT items (fixtures → story A/fe57; collapse appliers → story D/33d0; telemetry → story E/2359) consistent with the roster: AC3/SC2 → 33d0; AC4/SC3 → 2359; AC5/SC4 → 822a; fixtures/fake foundation → fe57; verified-fake honesty → 59ef.

4-bucket for this pairing: AC1 + AC2 + SC1 FULLY by THIS child; SC5 posture contributed; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below — and 8937 is the repeatedly-named natural home).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (re-cross-checked against the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child; already tracked across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57 — restated here for this pairing's record because 8937 is the named natural home):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this keystone (8937 AC4), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 8937 AC4 "All assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), not whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count" is self-scoped to THIS story's own contract/round-trip tests; siblings 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57's "re-capture procedure so fixtures … do not rot into change-detectors" are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests/fixtures; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: THIS keystone 8937 — e.g. add an AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 8937 itself for its owned criteria (AC1/AC2/SC1 fully covered); the single absence above is the only uncovered parent criterion across the whole roster (AC6), surfaced here because 8937 is the named natural home.
